papers.title_en: Learning dynamical systems from noisy data with Weak-form Kernel Ridge Regression
papers.authors: Max Kreider, John Harlim, Daning Huang
papers.evidence: 数据驱动动力学系统学习为李雅普诺夫函数发现提供基础
papers.tldr: 从含噪测量数据学习复杂动力系统是科学计算中的挑战，标准核岭回归对噪声敏感。弱形式核岭回归（WKRR）利用弱形式固有的滤波机制，结合偏差-方差误差分解，在混沌系统（64维）和真实流体数据（15000维）中均显著优于基线方法。该方法简单易实现，兼具噪声鲁棒性与高预测精度。
papers.abstract_en: Accurate prediction of complex dynamical systems from noisy measurements remains a significant challenge in scientific computing. Kernel ridge regression learning strategies are often effective when applied to clean data, but have limited success with noisy data. Recent work has observed that a weak formulation can act to filter noisy data, and different learning strategies have achieved increased noise robustness with a weak-form framework. In this manuscript, we give an overview of the filtering mechanism behind the weak formulation and provide a bias-variance error decomposition. Using these insights, we combine a weak formulation with a kernel learning strategy to propose Weak-form Kernel Ridge Regression (WKRR) for learning dynamical systems. The proposed framework is simple to implement, effective for both clean and noisy data, and outperforms several baseline methods. We demonstrate the performance of WKRR on chaotic benchmark systems in up to 64 dimensions, as well as 15,000-dimensional real-world fluid data.
